clusters.label: The universe is one connected whole
clusters.desc: Everything that exists forms a single ordered, living system governed by one reason, so each part is bound to and follows from every other.

clusters.label: Repay wrong with generosity
clusters.desc: Instead of retaliating, hoarding, or excusing yourself, give freely, forgive, repent, and treat others' dignity and property as your own.

clusters.label: Suffering comes from your judgments
clusters.desc: Events themselves cannot harm you; distress arises from the opinions you add to them, and you can withhold those opinions and simply act rightly now.

clusters.label: Wrongdoing destroys the wrongdoer
clusters.desc: Good and evil rebound on the person who does them, so the wicked are trapped by their own schemes while the upright endure.

clusters.label: Ultimate reality escapes the senses
clusters.desc: What underlies everything cannot be seen, argued to, or thought, and is found only by turning attention inward or entering the standpoint of what is observed.

clusters.label: Giving multiplies, hoarding wastes
clusters.desc: Wealth held back or endlessly craved rots and yields nothing, while what is shared, used, and worked for sustains both giver and community.

clusters.label: Love is the highest path
clusters.desc: Wholehearted love, not sentiment but self-surrender and devotion to what cannot die, is the instrument through which ultimate reality is known and reached.

clusters.label: Speak, listen, or walk away
clusters.desc: Words are wasted on those who will not hear and withheld wrongly from those who would, so live what you profess and leave company that cannot be reached.

clusters.label: Keep worthy company, act sincerely
clusters.desc: Ground yourself in loyalty and honesty, treat everyone with the respect you would give an honored guest, and attach yourself to good people.

clusters.label: Character shows without display
clusters.desc: A settled person embodies their principles rather than talking about them, stays inwardly content and unshaken, and does good without seeking notice.

clusters.label: Understanding frees you from death
clusters.desc: What becomes of you after death follows from what you have done and understood, and realizing the deathless reality releases you from the cycle of rebirth.

clusters.label: Character outranks worldly success
clusters.desc: The good person is unmoved by praise or blame and cares for principle, while the petty person chases comfort and favor and steadily declines.

clusters.label: The mind rules the body
clusters.desc: You are not your body, senses, or feelings but the ruling mind, which harms itself only by its own judgments and is your best friend when governed.

clusters.label: Choose pleasure by clear judgment
clusters.desc: Pleasure and pain touch only the senses, so weigh them by what they lead to rather than by how they feel, and let no craving disturb the governing mind.

clusters.label: Ignored warnings bring their reckoning
clusters.desc: Those who mock, dismiss, or joke away the truth are abandoned to the consequences of their own deeds, which rise up to surround them.

clusters.label: Wisdom is worth more than wealth
clusters.desc: Wisdom is the one possession that repays every cost paid for it, outlasting money, pleasure, and status.

clusters.label: Power works through yielding
clusters.desc: Real influence comes from taking no credit, forcing nothing, and staying low, because opposites define each other and the soft outlasts the hard.

clusters.label: What you receive, you owe
clusters.desc: Anyone who accepts mercy, patience, or a benefit and then refuses to extend it forfeits what they were given, and no commitment is real unless you carry its full cost yourself.

clusters.label: Accept events, act rightly anyway
clusters.desc: Whatever happens serves the whole and cannot stop you from being just, so meet each circumstance as the material your reason and kindness are given to work with.

clusters.label: Real knowledge hides in plain sight
clusters.desc: What is most worth knowing is concealed from the powerful and the confident, and is often already present inside the person who claims not to have it.

clusters.label: Lasting joy comes from within
clusters.desc: Deep and unlosable delight is found in the self, in doing good, and in attentive reflection, not in pleasures that can be taken away.

clusters.label: Nothing substitutes for the real thing
clusters.desc: A thing has a definite nature that makes it what it is, and once that quality is gone no imitation, argument, or replacement will serve.

clusters.label: No one escapes final accounting
clusters.desc: Every person will be judged by a reckoning that cannot be bribed, evaded, or transferred to anyone else, with their own record standing as the witness.

clusters.label: Study to become better, not impressive
clusters.desc: Learning is worthless as ornament or credential and only counts when it changes the character of the person doing it.

clusters.label: Character shows in sustained conduct
clusters.desc: Genuine goodness reveals itself through humility, reliability in small things, and steadiness maintained over long stretches rather than in bursts.

clusters.label: Learning is renewed and handed on
clusters.desc: Knowledge is passed down, fades, and must be recovered, so the real teacher turns the old into something new and the real student surpasses the teacher.

clusters.label: Integrity outlasts dishonest wealth
clusters.desc: Money got by greed, bribery, or exploitation collapses on the person who holds it, while honest poverty and generous giving steadily build a life worth having.

clusters.label: Everything perishes, even your name
clusters.desc: Bodies, fortunes, cities, and the memory of the dead all dissolve, so nothing worldly deserves to be clung to.

clusters.label: Do good without seeking credit
clusters.desc: A good act is already its own complete reward, so demanding recognition, repayment, or gratitude cancels its worth.

clusters.label: Rulers lead by self-restraint
clusters.desc: Authority holds only when the ruler corrects himself first, provides for the people, uses capable subordinates, and governs so lightly that force is unnecessary.

clusters.label: Denying truth to protect yourself
clusters.desc: People reject a message not because the evidence fails but because accepting it would cost them something, so they invent charges of trickery, madness, or forgery to excuse themselves.

clusters.label: Marks of the truly noble
clusters.desc: The genuinely noble person is the one who has cut every attachment, let anger and pride fall away, wants nothing from any world, and reached the end of suffering.

clusters.label: Guard what you say
clusters.desc: Careless and excessive speech traps and destroys the speaker, while restraint, accuracy, and weight in words protect a life and reveal wisdom.

clusters.label: Judge others gently
clusters.desc: Someone who wrongs you acts on what looks right to him and harms himself by his error, so examine your own faults, withhold confident verdicts, and pity rather than resent him.

clusters.label: Freedom from craving
clusters.desc: Letting go of desire, doubt, attachment, and the sense of "mine" releases a person from suffering and fear here and now.

clusters.label: Faith proved by conduct
clusters.desc: What counts is acting rightly and following what you know to be true, since outward profession, birth, and rules mean nothing without deeds behind them.

clusters.label: The wise and the foolish
clusters.desc: The wise take advice, restrain their anger, and hold knowledge back for the right moment, while fools trust themselves, broadcast their folly, and talk their way into ruin.

clusters.label: Wisdom holds opposites together
clusters.desc: Reality moves by reversal and balance, so what is grasped is lost, what is reduced grows, and understanding shows itself in restraint rather than argument or control.

clusters.label: Achievement without meaning is empty
clusters.desc: Wealth, long life, knowledge, and everything a person builds are worth nothing without love, satisfaction, and understanding of what one is for.

clusters.label: Peace of the settled mind
clusters.desc: Those who govern themselves and accept whatever befalls them find a lasting contentment that neither sorrow nor joy can disturb.

clusters.label: Everything is one reality
clusters.desc: Every god, force, and living thing is an aspect of a single underlying reality that contains all things, dwells in every heart, and is not contained by anything.

clusters.label: Creation points to its source
clusters.desc: The order of nature, the making of the human body, and everything that exists bear witness to a single creating power for anyone willing to reflect.

clusters.label: Your deeds return to you
clusters.desc: What a person gives, endures, or inflicts comes back to them multiplied, and no one is defrauded of what their actions have earned.

clusters.label: Hold on to wisdom
clusters.desc: Acquire understanding above everything else, accept instruction rather than refusing it, and guard good teaching until it becomes part of you.

clusters.label: Death is nothing to fear
clusters.desc: Death is a natural process stripped of the terrors we add to it, so fear disgrace instead, live as though each moment could be the last, and hold to virtue.

clusters.label: Wisdom is taught and received
clusters.desc: Wisdom passes from teacher to willing listener through well-timed, gracious speech, and it takes root in whoever seeks it and stores it up.

clusters.label: Blind to your own faults
clusters.desc: People see others' small flaws clearly while hiding and rationalizing their own much larger ones, and self-improvement begins with looking inward instead of outward.

clusters.label: Everything changes and dissolves
clusters.desc: Nature endlessly transforms the same matter into new forms, so nothing is truly lost, nothing is new, and death is neither strange nor to be feared.

clusters.label: A community bound by something greater
clusters.desc: People with different gifts form one body held together by a shared source larger than any of them, and small hidden influences slowly transform the whole.

clusters.label: Real virtue over its appearance
clusters.desc: Practicing goodness requires counting its real cost and doing what your nature demands whether or not anyone sees, rather than performing seriousness for an audience.

clusters.label: Unchecked desire destroys you
clusters.desc: Appetite, arrogance, and the pursuit of pleasure without restraint obscure a person's judgment and ruin the very one who indulges them.

clusters.label: Guard what enters your mind
clusters.desc: Watch your thoughts and impulses closely, refuse to be jerked around by anger, craving, or dread, and let nothing outside you take the place of what matters most.

clusters.label: Act without attachment to results
clusters.desc: Work done as an offering, without ego or grasping at outcomes, binds no one, and nothing external can harm a person who offers it nothing to seize.

clusters.label: Wrongdoing harms the wrongdoer most
clusters.desc: The harm a person plots for others originates in himself, corrupts him, and returns to destroy him.

clusters.label: Things you must not do
clusters.desc: Refuse to scheme against the trusting, gloat over an enemy's fall, envy those who prosper by force, or let others pressure you into softening what is right.

clusters.label: Honesty and humility bring standing
clusters.desc: Integrity, humility, and welcoming correction protect a person and earn real honor, while arrogance, malice, and deceit bring collapse and disgrace.

clusters.label: Judge effort against real conditions
clusters.desc: Effective action means reckoning the full cost beforehand, respecting the limits of your competence and the timing of circumstances, and neither overshooting nor falling short.

clusters.label: Train attention through steady practice
clusters.desc: Repeatedly returning the wandering mind to one thing, in the right conditions and with disciplined speech and action, brings a steady joy nothing external can reach.

clusters.label: The humble are lifted up
clusters.desc: Those who exalt themselves and refuse uncomfortable truth are brought low, while the humble, the merciful, and the powerless are vindicated and given rest.

clusters.label: Do the work in front of you
clusters.desc: Live each moment doing your present task honestly, sociably, and without vanity or complaint, since nothing outside you can prevent you from being good.
